key_insight: TopoAgent replaces brittle linear multimodal reasoning with a dynamically evolving DAG of isolated atomic subproblems, improving robustness for scientific tasks.
engineering_relevance: The approach is relevant to engineering AI workflows because it can reduce context contamination and improve reliability when solving complex, tool-using analysis tasks.
method: Decompose a complex query into visually grounded atomic units.
Arrange atoms in a Directed Acyclic Graph to enforce dependency-aware context isolation.
Use adaptive atomic fission to split bottleneck atoms at runtime when tools are insufficient.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive evaluations across mathematics, physics, and chemistry benchmarks, where TopoAgent outperforms state-of-the-art linear agent frameworks.
watch_item: Runtime graph splitting and strict dependency management may add implementation complexity and require careful tool boundary design.
paper_title: TopoAgent: A Self-Evolving Topological Agent for Multimodal Scientific Reasoning
